subject: Cloud Computing
title: Global Databases
description: A fast breakdown of Global Databases for builders.

hook.text: Google Spanner is a database that spans the entire planet. It uses atomic clocks to keep data consistent.

buildup.text: Global databases replicate data across continents, giving every user fast, local access with strong consistency.

discovery.text: Spanner, CockroachDB, and Cosmos DB offer globally distributed databases, solving the latency vs. consistency dilemma.

twist.text: Global databases are expensive and complex. Most applications don't need planetary-scale data distribution.

climax.text: They're built for apps with truly global users: messaging, gaming, and financial services that need speed everywhere.

punchline.text: Global databases span the planet. Most apps don't need that yet.

quiz.question: How does Google Spanner maintain consistency across continents?
options: It uses atomic clocks to precisely synchronize data across global replicas
It only allows writes from one continent at a time
Data is stored in a single data center and cached everywhere else
